$schema_note: MACHINE-CHECKED SNAPSHOT CONTRACT for the Anthology Engine (Skill 59) Convert and Flow (GoHighLevel / LeadConnector v2) snapshot. This is the fixture that pins WHAT the exported GHL snapshot must contain so onboarding is a ONE-IMPORT step (mirrors Skill 38's references/snapshot-inbound-workflow-fixture.json + qc-23-key-bodies.sh). It is cross-checked by scripts/qc-snapshot-contract.sh against the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH config/field-map.json (pipeline + the 28 custom fields) and against config/engine-config.template.json (the intake route path + credential labels): if the engine's expected pipeline name, stage names, or field keys/types drift from this fixture, the CI drift gate FAILS before the stale snapshot can ship. The fixture NEVER carries a real token or a real hook URL -- every per-client value is a clearly-labeled REPLACE-ME placeholder, filled per install by scripts/anthology_snapshot.py provision-custom-values (the same never-a-real-token rule as Skill 38). The client-facing platform name is Convert and Flow everywhere; nothing Anthropic appears in this file.
snapshot_version_note: Date-stamped name the operator gives the exported snapshot (Settings -> Snapshots -> Create Snapshot). Bump it whenever the pipeline/fields/tags/forms/workflows below change and the snapshot is re-cut. scripts/anthology_snapshot.py stamp-version writes this string into the per-box marker $STATE_DIR/snapshot-version.json so a box records which snapshot it was provisioned from.

source_template_location.$note: The snapshot is CUT ONCE from the operator's OWN Anthology template Convert and Flow location, NEVER from a client account. This is operator infrastructure config (an operator-owned template location id, not client PII and not a secret -- it carries no provider-key prefix). See references/anthology-snapshot-guide.md for the full cut-and-import runbook.
source_template_location.platform: Convert and Flow (white-label of a LeadConnector v2 instance: services.leadconnectorhq.com, Version 2021-07-28)

credential_labels.$note: Mirror of config/engine-config.template.json; the drift gate asserts these equal the engine-config labels so scripts/anthology_snapshot.py (which resolves the client PIT + location + hook secret BY LABEL, never by value) can never drift from the engine's own label contract.

pipeline.$note: GoHighLevel / Convert and Flow exposes NO public v2 API to CREATE a pipeline -- pipelines are UI-only. The snapshot MUST pre-create this pipeline with the EXACT name and the EXACT stage names below; onboarding (provision-anthology-client.sh step 3) then FINDS it BY NAME and BINDS it through the client's OWN PIT. Both the name and every stage name are load-bearing; they must byte-equal config/field-map.json pipeline.standard_pipeline_name + standard_stages (drift gate assertion).
pipeline.name: Anthology Engine

custom_fields.$note: The 28 CONTACT custom fields the snapshot must carry (model=contact), byte-identical to config/field-map.json provisioning.fields. 27 are LARGE_TEXT free-text keys; exactly 1 is SINGLE_OPTIONS (the U8 cover choice, whose picklist options are the four cover-style NAMES). fieldKey derivation law: the API derives fieldKey = 'contact.' + <create_name>; the snapshot's field NAMEs must be exactly the create_name values so the derived keys land byte-equal. onboarding step 2 (anthology_registry.py provision-fields) is create-or-verify + idempotent: it verifies each key by name and creates only the missing ones, so a snapshot that already carries all 28 makes step 2 a verify-only no-op.

location_custom_values.$note: The engine's PYTHON code reads ZERO location custom-values (every dynamic value it reads/writes is a contact custom field or a contact tag). These 4 custom-values are the SNAPSHOT'S OWN plumbing: they are consumed by the snapshot's tag->notification GHL workflow below (its Custom Webhook action fires the client's intake hook, its email/SMS templates greet the producer), exactly as Skill 38's snapshot carries openclaw_hook_url / openclaw_hooks_bearer. The snapshot ships them as REPLACE-ME placeholders (never a real value); scripts/anthology_snapshot.py provision-custom-values fills them per install, idempotently (GET-check, create-only-missing else update). engine_reads_custom_values is therefore false, but the SNAPSHOT still requires them so the notification automation works in the client account.
location_custom_values.consumed_by: the snapshot's tag->notification GHL workflow (NOT the engine python)

required.name: Anthology Webhook Url
required.fill_source: the client's real intake hook URL (route_path /hooks/anthology-intake on the box's public hostname)

required.name: Anthology Hook Secret
required.fill_source: the Authorization header value the GHL workflow sends to the client's hook; resolved BY LABEL from ANTHOLOGY_INTAKE_HOOK_SECRET and written into the client's OWN GHL account; the value is NEVER printed to any operator surface

required.name: Producer
required.placeholder: REPLACE-ME with the producer (box owner) display name

tags.$note: The release-tag bus (namespace anthology-release-* plus anthology-delivered). Tags are NOT provisioned by the API -- GHL creates a tag on first add-tags call (caf_delivery.py CafClient.add_tag), so the engine works whether or not the snapshot pre-declares them. Seeding all 8 slugs in the snapshot is recommended for cleanliness AND so the tag->notification workflow can pre-bind its trigger. status: LIVE = the producer gate exists today and the slug fires; WIRED-AHEAD = slug defined, producer gate not yet in GATE_BY_CURSOR; DOCTRINE = named in code comments/self-test only, not yet shelled.

forms.$note: The provisioner does NOT auto-create forms; forms are UI/snapshot-created like the pipeline. Every form carries the universal hidden-field contract (contact_id, anthology_id, stage), re-stamped on gate re-entry. The query param on the minted intake link is EXACTLY anthology_id, never anthology_active_id.

required.note: The intake front door. The minted link is <forms_base_url>/widget/form/<universal_intake_form_id>?anthology_id=<minted>, built by anthology_book.py with a fleet-default hosted-form domain + a single universal author-intake form id baked in.

workflows.$note: The single biggest thing a real snapshot must ADD beyond field/pipeline/form parity: the tag-triggered notification automation. The engine emits anthology-release-* tags but the GHL automation that turns a release tag into the client-facing email + SMS is NOT shipped in the repo (scan-no-json-exports.sh bans workflow JSON exports; the only n8n asset present is the unrelated Drive credential broker). This block is a CONTRACT DESCRIPTION (trigger + ordered actions), deliberately NOT an n8n/GHL workflow JSON export (no connections/pinData node graph), so it documents what the operator builds ONCE in the template location before cutting the snapshot without tripping the export-leak scanner.

tag_to_notification.purpose: When an anthology-release-* tag lands on a contact, greet the producer/participant and (optionally) call the box's intake hook.

actions.note: URL + Authorization are REPLACE-ME custom values, never inlined tokens (Skill 38 never-a-real-token rule).
optional; ships DISABLED as an example, enabled per client need

tag_to_notification.ships_disabled_note: Like Skill 38's lifecycle tag automations, the notification workflow ships DISABLED so the operator enables only what the client needs; the 3 LIVE slug triggers are enabled first, the 3 wired-ahead slugs when those gates ship.

intake.$note: Cross-checked against config/engine-config.template.json intake.route_path + intake.route_secret_label so the snapshot's webhook custom value points at the same route the box's gateway actually serves.

rejected_mechanisms.reason: This fleet's topology is EACH-CLIENT-OWNS-THEIR-OWN-GHL: a client's Convert and Flow location is owned by the client under their own agency, not a subaccount of a BlackCEO agency. GoHighLevel snapshot PUSH/LOAD across agency boundaries into a location the operator does not own under their agency is almost certainly impossible (and would require agency-level ownership the operator does not have over a client-owned location). The ONLY realistic path is the MANUAL IMPORT of the exported snapshot into the client's own location (operator-run, Settings -> Snapshots -> Import), followed by the per-client custom-value fill + verify built here. If a future fleet topology ever nests client locations under a BlackCEO agency, revisit.
